fields.timestamp_end: ISO8601 UTC — when service restored (null if ongoing)
fields.resolution: free text — how it was fixed
fields.mttr_minutes: integer — mean time to recover (same as duration for single incidents)
fields.recurrence: boolean — has this type occurred before?
fields.notion_page_id: string — linked Notion page ID (null if not synced)

incidents.trigger: Planned circuit shutdown — power trip on OC1 host circuit
incidents.rca: Deliberate power circuit shutdown (planned maintenance). OC1 (Mac mini) shut down ungracefully. On restart, FileVault full-disk encryption required manual login before OpenClaw gateway and agents could resume. No data loss. No config corruption detected.
incidents.resolution: Manual physical login at OC1 console to unlock FileVault. Gateway and all agents resumed automatically post-login.

incidents.trigger: openclaw update command triggered SIGKILL — gateway self-restarted, session context lost
incidents.rca: Platform update (openclaw update 2026.4.24) triggered self-restart via SIGKILL. Session context not flushed beforehand. Ken required double memory recall to restore Sprint 2 context. Root cause: no pre-risky-op checkpoint protocol existed.
incidents.resolution: Session restored via session file forensics. Pre-risky-op checkpoint rule created. Memory search doctor error fixed (OLLAMA_HOST + remote config). Gateway restarted clean.
incidents.prevention: Pre-risky-op checkpoint rule (NON-NEGOTIABLE) — flush all context before any OpenClaw update/restart

incidents.trigger: Gateway restart (for memory fix) hit ENOTEMPTY on plugin-runtime-deps — crash loop for ~116 min
incidents.rca: Earlier openclaw update SIGKILL left partial plugin-sdk directory in plugin-runtime-deps. Gateway restart triggered re-extraction which failed with ENOTEMPTY (cannot overwrite non-empty dir). LaunchAgent kept retrying — gateway crashed on every startup attempt. Eventually resolved on a later LaunchAgent retry.
incidents.resolution: Gateway self-recovered via LaunchAgent retry at ~21:10. Telegram confirmed working 21:21. No manual intervention possible remotely due to FileVault restriction.
incidents.prevention: Pre-risky-op checkpoint updated: clear stale plugin-runtime-deps before any gateway restart. Add to PVT checklist (US21).

incidents.resolution: Closed — test entry for QW-2 severity flag validation. Not a real incident.
Closed — test entry for PIR auto-trigger validation. Not a real incident.

incidents.title: WebSocket 1006/1000 + Node.js V8 worker crash + qqbot ENOTEMPTY
incidents.cause: Node.js worker thread crashed (V8 ValueDeserializer structured-clone error). Heavy sub-agent load in rapid succession: blog rewrite + Lex review + governance policy enforcement. qqbot ENOTEMPTY on stale plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-unknown-48e1596a6b24 compounded recovery cycle.
incidents.resolution: Gateway auto-recovered (HTTP 200 confirmed). Stale plugin-runtime-deps cleared. No data loss. No restart required.
incidents.impact: WebSocket 1006 (abnormal drop) + 1000 (clean reconnect). ~1 min disruption to webchat. All sub-agents completed successfully.
incidents.prevention: Avoid spawning multiple large sub-agents simultaneously in final session hour. Pre-risky-op step 5 (clear stale plugin-runtime-deps) needs to run more proactively.
